header.headline: Hungarian Citizenship & Naturalization
header.description: A simplified guide to the honosítás (simplified naturalization) process and how the Honorary Consulate supports your application.

intro.heading: What Is Simplified Naturalization?
intro.body: Hungary's simplified naturalization program (honosítás egyszerűsített eljárással) allows ethnic Hungarians and their descendants outside Hungary to apply for Hungarian citizenship without a residency requirement. If your parents, grandparents, or earlier ancestors were Hungarian citizens, you may be eligible.

items.title: Gather Ancestry Documentation
items.body: Collect birth certificates, marriage certificates, and any documents that trace your Hungarian lineage. Documents must show the chain from you to a Hungarian ancestor.

items.title: Authenticate Your Documents
items.body: American documents (birth, marriage certificates) must be authenticated by the Honorary Consul before submission. Hungarian documents may need copy authentication.

items.title: Submit to the Embassy in Washington DC
items.body: Citizenship applications are submitted to the Embassy of Hungary in Washington DC — not to the honorary consul. The Embassy handles all processing.

items.title: Language and Constitutional Knowledge
items.body: Applicants must demonstrate basic Hungarian language ability and knowledge of Hungarian history and constitution. The Honorary Consul can provide guidance but does not administer the test.

items.title: Oath of Citizenship
items.body: If approved, you will take the oath of citizenship — either in Hungary or at a Hungarian diplomatic mission. Sylvia can advise on the process but does not administer oaths.

cannotDo.heading: What the Honorary Consul Cannot Do
items: Grant, deny, or appeal citizenship decisions
Provide legal advice on citizenship eligibility

canDo.heading: How the Honorary Consul Can Help
items: Authenticate birth and marriage certificates for submission
Certify copies of Hungarian ancestry documents
Explain the general process and required documents
Refer you to resources and contacts at the Embassy

cta.heading: Ready to Start?
cta.body: Book an appointment to authenticate your ancestry documents and receive guidance on the application process.
